Reference to the originating purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. Enables three-way match (PO, GR, invoice) in accounts payable and ERP procure-to-pay workflow.

description: Business category of the goods or services being invoiced. description: Bank routing identifier for payment processing — either SWIFT/BIC code for international wire transfers or ABA routing number for US domestic ACH payments. Required for accurate payment routing in the AP settlement process.
reference: SWIFT BIC Standard (ISO 9362); ABA Routing Number Standard; PCI DSS Requirement 3; SAP S/4HANA (LFBK-BANKL)

description: First line of the supplier's registered billing address. Used for invoice processing, payment remittance, and accounts payable (AP) in SAP S/4HANA. Required for PCI DSS-compliant payment processing and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1-STRAS); PCI DSS Requirement 3; SOX Section 404

description: City component of the supplier's registered billing address. Used in conjunction with billing_address_line1 for complete address resolution in AP payment processing and tax jurisdiction determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1-ORT01); ISO 3166-2 Subdivision Codes

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the supplier's billing address country. Drives tax jurisdiction rules, currency defaults, and cross-border trade compliance in the ERP procure-to-pay process.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA (LFA1-LAND1); WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement

description: Postal or ZIP code of the supplier's billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, carrier rate determination, and address validation in the procure-to-pay workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1-PSTLZ); Universal Postal Union (UPU) Standards

description: Expiry date of the current master vendor agreement or supplier contract. Triggers contract renewal workflows and procurement alerts when approaching expiry. Nullable for open-ended evergreen contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Outline Agreement (EKKO-KDATB); SOX Section 404 Contract Controls

description: Effective start date of the current master vendor agreement or supplier contract. Defines the period during which negotiated pricing, payment terms, and SLAs are binding. Used in SAP S/4HANA outline agreements and procurement scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Outline Agreement (EKKO-KDATB); SOX Section 404 Contract Controls

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the supplier is legally registered and primarily operates. Used for import/export compliance, tariff classification, and WTO trade regulation adherence.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO Rules of Origin; U.S. Customs and Border Protection

description: Expiry date of the supplier's CPSC product safety certification. Used to enforce product safety compliance gating in the Product Information Management System (PIM) catalog onboarding workflow.
reference: CPSC Consumer Product Safety Act; CPSC 16 CFR Part 1107 (Third Party Testing)

business_glossary_term: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether the supplier's products meet Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) safety standards. Required for suppliers providing consumer goods sold on the e-commerce marketplace. Non-compliant suppliers are blocked from product catalog onboarding.
reference: CPSC Consumer Product Safety Act (CPSA); CPSC 16 CFR Parts 1107 and 1109

description: Timestamp when the supplier master record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the supplier lifecycle. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset per platform convention.
reference: ISO 8601 Date/Time Standard; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability

description: Standard supplier lead time in calendar days from Purchase Order (PO) placement to goods receipt at the warehouse. Used as the default for inventory replenishment planning and MOQ calculations when no SKU-specific lead time is defined. Feeds into the WMS receiving schedule and ERP supply chain planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MRP Lead Time (MARC-WEBAZ); WMS Receiving Planning; Procurement SLA Management

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether the supplier has signed a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) and is confirmed GDPR compliant for handling EU personal data. Mandatory for suppliers processing customer data on behalf of the e-commerce platform under GDPR Article 28.
reference: GDPR Article 28 (Data Processing Agreement); GDPR Article 32 (Security of Processing); Electronic Commerce Directive (EU)

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between the buyer and supplier for goods delivery. Drives logistics cost allocation, insurance requirements, and customs documentation in the TMS and ERP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement; SAP S/4HANA Purchasing (EKKO-INCO1)

description: Indicates whether the supplier holds a recognized diversity certification (e.g., minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, small business). Tracked for supplier diversity program reporting, ESG compliance, and government contract requirements.
reference: FTC Supplier Diversity Standards; U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) 8(a) Program; ESG Reporting Frameworks

description: Indicates whether this supplier is designated as a preferred vendor in the approved vendor list (AVL). Preferred vendors receive priority in automated PO sourcing and are eligible for blanket purchase agreements. Managed in SAP S/4HANA vendor evaluation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Evaluation (ME61); Procurement Approved Vendor List Policy

description: Expiry date of the supplier's ISO certification. Procurement compliance workflows use this date to trigger re-certification reminders and to flag suppliers with lapsed certifications for suspension review.

Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid ISO certification (e.g., ISO 9001 Quality Management, ISO 27001 Information Security, ISO 14001 Environmental Management). Drives compliance gating in the supplier onboarding workflow.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management; ISO 27001:2022 Information Security; ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the supplier master record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the ERP-to-lakehouse ETL pipeline and for audit trail compliance under SOX and GDPR accountability requirements.
reference: ISO 8601 Date/Time Standard; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; ETL CDC Pattern

description: Full registered legal name of the supplier entity as recorded in official business registration documents. Used for contract execution, tax filings, and regulatory compliance. Must match the name on vendor contracts and invoices.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1-NAME1); FTC Consumer Protection Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) — the minimum number of units the supplier requires per purchase order line. Enforced during PO creation in the ERP to prevent under-minimum orders. Critical for inventory planning and procurement cost optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record (EINA-MINBM); Procurement Best Practices

description: Date on which the supplier was formally approved and activated in the procurement system following completion of the onboarding workflow (KYC, compliance checks, contract execution). Marks the start of the active vendor relationship.
reference: Seller Management Portal Onboarding Workflow; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master Activation

description: Current lifecycle state of the supplier in the procurement onboarding and vendor management workflow. Pending: application submitted. Under Review: compliance and due diligence in progress. Approved: cleared for procurement. Active: currently transacting. Suspended: temporarily blocked. Terminated: relationship ended.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Lifecycle Management; Seller Management Portal Onboarding Workflow

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the supplier invoices and is paid (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Drives FX conversion in SAP S/4HANA AP and financial settlement. Essential for multi-currency procurement operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1-WAERS); SOX Financial Reporting

description: Standard payment terms code agreed with the supplier, defining the payment due date and any early payment discount conditions (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 meaning 2% discount if paid within 10 days, net due in 30). Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment terms (ZTERM). Critical for cash flow management and AP settlement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Payment Terms (T052-ZTERM); SOX Section 404 AP Controls; GAAP Accounts Payable Standards

business_glossary_term: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether the supplier is currently PCI DSS compliant, relevant for suppliers handling payment card data or integrated into the payment processing ecosystem. Required for suppliers in the Payment Gateway and Processing Platform integration.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 (PCI Security Standards Council); PCI SSC Compliance Requirements

description: Expiry date of the supplier's PCI DSS compliance certification. Annual re-assessment is required per PCI SSC standards. Used to trigger compliance renewal workflows and to block non-compliant suppliers from payment data integrations.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.3.2; PCI Security Standards Council Annual Assessment

description: Business email address of the primary contact person at the supplier. Used for PO transmission, invoice correspondence, and procurement workflow notifications. Subject to GDPR legitimate interest basis for B2B processing.
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(f); CCPA Section 1798.140; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Communication (LFA1-SMTP_ADDR)

description: Full name of the primary business contact person at the supplier organization. Used for procurement communications, PO acknowledgments, and relationship management. Not a personal consumer — represents a B2B contact.
reference: GDPR Article 6 (legitimate interest for B2B contacts); SAP S/4HANA Contact Person (KNVK)

description: Business phone number of the primary contact at the supplier organization. Used for urgent procurement escalations, delivery coordination, and supplier relationship management. Stored in E.164 international format.
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(f); ITU-T E.164 International Telephone Numbering Plan; SAP S/4HANA (LFA1-TELF1)

description: Business role or function of the primary contact person at the supplier. Determines routing of procurement communications (e.g., POs to account manager, invoices to finance contact, shipment queries to logistics coordinator).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Contact Person Function (KNVK-PAFKT); Procurement SLA Management

description: Official government-issued business registration or incorporation number for the supplier entity. Used for legal verification, KYC (Know Your Customer) compliance, and regulatory filings. Format varies by country of registration.
reference: ISO 6523; FTC Business Registration; GDPR Article 13 (data controller identification)

description: Categorical risk classification derived from the supplier risk assessment process. Low: minimal supply chain risk. Medium: moderate risk requiring monitoring. High: significant risk requiring mitigation plans. Critical: immediate action required. Used to trigger procurement escalation workflows.
reference: ISO 31000 Risk Management; NIST Cybersecurity Framework SP 800-161 (Supply Chain Risk)

description: Composite risk assessment score for the supplier on a 0.00–100.00 scale, where higher values indicate greater risk. Incorporates financial stability, geopolitical exposure, compliance history, and delivery performance. Used by procurement to prioritize supplier audits and diversification strategies.
reference: ISO 31000 Risk Management; NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Procurement Risk Management Best Practices

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned to the supplier for cross-system identification. Used in Purchase Orders (POs), EDI transactions, and ERP integrations. Maps to the SAP S/4HANA vendor account number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1-LIFNR); ISO 6523 Party Identification

description: Procurement tier classification indicating the strategic importance and relationship depth with the supplier. Strategic: top-tier partners with deep integration and long-term contracts. Preferred: vetted suppliers with negotiated terms. Approved: qualified suppliers on the approved vendor list. Transactional: one-off or spot-buy suppliers.
reference: Procurement Best Practices; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Classification

description: Classification of the supplier by their role in the supply chain. Manufacturer: produces goods directly. Distributor: resells from manufacturers. Wholesaler: bulk reseller. 3PL (Third-Party Logistics): logistics provider. Dropshipper: ships directly to end customer. Marketplace Seller: sells via the e-commerce marketplace platform.
reference: Procurement Domain Classification; WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement

description: Government-issued tax identification number for the supplier, such as EIN (Employer Identification Number) in the US, VAT number in the EU, or GST number in other jurisdictions. Required for tax reporting, invoice validation, and SOX-compliant financial controls.
reference: IRS EIN; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; SOX Section 302; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1-STCD1)

description: Operating or trade name (Doing Business As) of the supplier, which may differ from the legal name. Used in day-to-day procurement communications, catalog displays, and seller portal references.

VAT registration number for suppliers operating in VAT-applicable jurisdictions (primarily EU). Distinct from the general tax_id as it specifically governs VAT reclaim and cross-border EU trade compliance. Required for EU Electronic Commerce Directive compliance.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; Electronic Commerce Directive (EU); SAP S/4HANA (LFA1-STCD2)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each supplier performance scorecard record. Primary key for the supplier_performance data product in the procurement domain.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 - Data Element Naming and Definition

description: Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Category-level supplier performance scorecards are a standard procurement KPI report in e-commerce. Category managers evaluate supplier on-time delivery, defect rates, and fill rates within their spec
Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Performance monitoring: supplier performance scores are evaluated per seller to drive procurement decisions and SLA enforcement.
Reference to the supplier (vendor) being evaluated in this performance scorecard period. Links to the master supplier/vendor record managed in SAP S/4HANA Supplier Master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Supplier Master - Vendor Account

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: A supplier performance scorecard measures KPIs (on-time delivery, fill rate, defect rate) against SLA targets defined in the governing vendor contract. vendor_contract carries sla_on_time_delivery_pct

Average number of calendar days between purchase order placement and goods receipt for all POs evaluated during the period. Compared against contracted lead time to compute lead_time_adherence_rate and inform replenishment planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Planning - Lead Time Management

description: Procurement team's formal recommendation for the supplier's contract renewal based on this evaluation period's performance scorecard. Drives contract lifecycle management decisions in SAP S/4HANA. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Contract Management; Ecommerce Procurement Policy; SOX Internal Controls

description: The agreed-upon lead time in calendar days as specified in the supplier's active vendor contract or purchase agreement. Serves as the benchmark for lead_time_adherence_rate calculation and SLA compliance assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Contract Management; SLA (Service Level Agreement) Terms

description: Number of formal corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the supplier during the evaluation period due to quality, delivery, or compliance failures. High counts indicate systemic supplier issues requiring escalation or probation review.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Corrective Action Process; Ecommerce Supplier Quality Management

description: The timestamp when this supplier performance scorecard record was first created in the system. Audit trail field aligned with ISO 27001 data governance requirements and SOX internal control documentation.
reference: ISO 27001 - Audit Trail; SOX Section 404 - Record Keeping

business_glossary_term: Critical Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach Count
description: Number of high-severity SLA breaches that caused material business impact (e.g., stockouts, production stoppages, customer order cancellations) during the evaluation period. Subset of sla_breach_count; used for escalation and probation decisions.
reference: SLA (Service Level Agreement) Contract Terms; Ecommerce Procurement Escalation Policy

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the KPI data for this scorecard was primarily sourced. SAP_S4HANA for ERP-driven metrics; WMS for warehouse receiving data; TMS for delivery performance; SELLER_PORTAL for supplier self-reported data; MANUAL for procurement team input.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ERP; WMS (Warehouse Management System); TMS (Transportation Management System)

business_glossary_term: Defect Rate - Defective Parts Per Million (DPPM)
description: Number of defective units received per one million units delivered by the supplier during the evaluation period. Industry-standard quality metric (DPPM - Defective Parts Per Million) used to assess product quality compliance and drive supplier quality improvement programs.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Product Quality; Six Sigma DPPM Methodology; CPSC Product Safety Standards

description: Indicates whether the supplier has been enrolled in a formal supplier development program as a result of this evaluation period's performance. Typically triggered by bronze tier or probation classification.
reference: Ecommerce Supplier Development Program Policy; ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Improvement

description: Number of formal disputes raised by either Ecommerce or the supplier regarding PO terms, invoice discrepancies, or delivery claims during the evaluation period. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA dispute management module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Dispute Management; SOX Internal Controls

description: Percentage of shipments accompanied by complete and accurate documentation (packing lists, certificates of conformance, customs documents, safety data sheets) as required by contract and regulatory standards during the evaluation period.
reference: CPSC Product Safety Documentation; WTO International Trade Documentation; ISO 9001:2015

description: Free-text field for the procurement evaluator to capture qualitative observations, context, or exceptions that are not reflected in the quantitative KPI scores (e.g., force majeure events, supply chain disruptions, extraordinary performance).
The last calendar date of the performance evaluation window. Paired with evaluation_period_start_date to define the full measurement interval for all KPIs.
The first calendar date of the performance evaluation window. Used to define the measurement boundary for all KPIs captured in this scorecard.
Granularity of the performance evaluation cycle: monthly for operational monitoring, quarterly for strategic reviews, and annual for contract renewal decisions.

Percentage of ordered quantity actually fulfilled and shipped by the supplier during the evaluation period. Calculated as (units shipped / units ordered) × 100. Measures supplier's ability to meet demand without shortfalls, critical for inventory replenishment planning.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Order Fulfillment Performance; WMS Fill Rate Standards

description: Percentage of supplier invoices that matched the corresponding purchase order without discrepancies (price, quantity, or terms) during the evaluation period. Calculated as (accurate invoices / total invoices) × 100. Impacts accounts payable efficiency and procure-to-pay cycle time in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA AP Invoice Matching

description: The timestamp when this supplier performance scorecard record was most recently modified. Tracks revisions during the draft, review, and dispute resolution lifecycle stages. Required for data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 27001 - Audit Trail; SOX Section 404 - Record Keeping; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: Percentage of purchase orders where the supplier's actual lead time met or was within the contracted lead time during the evaluation period. Calculated as (orders within lead time / total orders) × 100. Critical for inventory replenishment planning and MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Lead Time Management; MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) Standards

description: Percentage of purchase orders where the supplier honored the contracted MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) terms during the evaluation period. Non-compliance may indicate supply constraints or contract violations requiring procurement intervention.
reference: MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) Contract Terms; SAP S/4HANA Procurement Policy

description: Percentage of purchase orders delivered by the supplier on or before the confirmed delivery date during the evaluation period. Calculated as (on-time deliveries / total deliveries) × 100. Key operational KPI for SLA compliance monitoring.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Delivery Performance; SLA (Service Level Agreement) Standards

description: Number of corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the supplier that remain unresolved as of the scorecard evaluation date. Unresolved CARs beyond SLA timelines may trigger escalation to probation tier.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Corrective Action Closure; Ecommerce Supplier Escalation Policy

description: Composite weighted performance score (0–100) aggregating all KPI dimensions (on-time delivery, fill rate, defect rate, invoice accuracy, lead time adherence, return rate, SLA compliance, NPS) per the procurement scoring model. Primary driver for performance tier classification and contract renewal decisions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Scorecard Methodology; Ecommerce Procurement Scoring Model

description: Classified performance tier assigned to the supplier based on the weighted overall performance score for the evaluation period. Gold = top-tier preferred supplier; Silver = satisfactory; Bronze = needs improvement; Probation = at risk of contract termination. Drives supplier development programs and contract renewal decisions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Classification; Ecommerce Procurement Policy

description: The supplier's performance tier from the immediately preceding evaluation period. Used to detect tier reclassification events and trigger automated supplier development workflows.
reference: Ecommerce Procurement Policy - Supplier Tier Reclassification

description: Percentage variance between the contracted unit price and the actual invoiced price across all POs in the evaluation period. Positive value indicates supplier overcharged vs. contract; negative indicates undercharge. Flags pricing compliance issues for AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Procurement Controls; SAP S/4HANA AP Price Variance

description: Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the internal procurement team rating the supplier's responsiveness, communication quality, and overall partnership experience during the evaluation period. Scale: -100 to +100. Captures qualitative relationship health not reflected in operational KPIs.
reference: NPS (Net Promoter Score) Methodology - Bain & Company; Ecommerce Procurement Relationship Management

description: Percentage of received units returned to the supplier due to defects, non-conformance, or incorrect shipments during the evaluation period. Calculated as (units returned / units received) × 100. Feeds into RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) processing and supplier quality programs.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Non-Conformance Management; RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Process

description: The date on which the finalized supplier performance scorecard was formally published and shared with the supplier. Marks the start of the supplier's response and dispute window.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Communication; Ecommerce Procurement Policy

description: Externally-known business identifier for this supplier performance scorecard record, used in procurement communications, supplier development meetings, and audit trails. Format: SPC-YYYY-MM-SUPPID.

Current lifecycle state of the supplier performance scorecard. Draft indicates data collection in progress; pending_review awaits procurement manager sign-off; approved is finalized; published is shared with supplier; disputed is under supplier challenge; closed is archived.

Total number of SLA (Service Level Agreement) violations recorded against the supplier during the evaluation period. Includes delivery, quality, and communication SLA breaches as defined in the vendor contract. Triggers penalty clauses and escalation workflows.
reference: SLA (Service Level Agreement) Contract Terms; ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Compliance

description: Indicates whether the supplier has formally acknowledged receipt and review of the published performance scorecard via the Seller Management Portal. Required for audit trail and dispute resolution process initiation.
reference: Ecommerce Seller Management Portal - Supplier Acknowledgement; ISO 9001:2015

description: The date on which the supplier formally acknowledged the performance scorecard in the Seller Management Portal. Null if supplier_acknowledged_flag is False. Used to track response timeliness.
reference: Ecommerce Seller Management Portal; ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Communication

description: Score (0–100) reflecting the supplier's adherence to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards and Ecommerce's supplier code of conduct during the evaluation period. Assessed via audits, certifications, and self-reported data. Increasingly required for regulatory and investor reporting.
reference: ISO 14001 - Environmental Management; UN Global Compact; Ecommerce Supplier Code of Conduct

description: Indicates whether the supplier's performance tier changed between the previous and current evaluation period. True triggers automated supplier development program enrollment or contract review workflows in the ERP.
reference: Ecommerce Procurement Policy - Automated Supplier Tier Reclassification

description: Total number of purchase orders (POs) included in the performance evaluation for this period. Provides the denominator context for rate-based KPIs and ensures statistical significance of the scorecard.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Purchase Order Management; PO (Purchase Order) Standards

description: Total quantity of units received from the supplier across all purchase orders during the evaluation period. Used as the volume denominator for defect rate, return rate, and fill rate calculations. Sourced from WMS (Warehouse Management System) receiving records.
reference: WMS (Warehouse Management System) - Receiving Records; ISO 9001:2015

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier price list record. Primary key for this entity in the procurement domain Silver layer.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 - Data Element Naming and Definition

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B e-commerce supports account-specific supplier pricing (negotiated rates for enterprise buyers). supplier_price_list already has segment_id for segment-level pricing, but account-level pricing is a
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price‑list maintenance needs cost‑center attribution for budgeting of negotiated pricing programs.
Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Ensures price‑list changes are reflected in the GL for expense forecasting and audit compliance.

Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Supplier price lists define the buy-side cost for products listed on the marketplace. Linking supplier_price_list to marketplace_listing enables real-time gross margin calculation (listing price minus
Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Enables segment‑specific pricing; the pricing engine selects a price list based on the customer.segment of the buyer.

Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Pricing: sellers receive negotiated price lists from suppliers; linking allows price‑list selection and margin calculations per seller.
Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this price list entry applies. Each price list record covers exactly one SKU.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (MM60)

description: Reference to the newer supplier_price_list record that replaced this one when status is 'superseded'. Enables version chain traversal for price history analysis and audit. Null if this is the current active version.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Versioning; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) who owns this price list. Links to the supplier master record in the procurement domain.
Reference to the vendor contract or purchasing outline agreement under which this price list is governed. Null if the price list is a standalone purchasing info record not tied to a formal contract.
The date on which this price list was formally approved by the authorized procurement officer. Required for SOX compliance audit trails and procurement governance reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls; ISO 9001 Document Approval; SAP S/4HANA MM - Release Strategy Date

description: The name or employee identifier of the procurement officer or manager who approved this price list for use in purchase order processing. Supports SOX procurement controls and audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting; SAP S/4HANA MM - Release Strategy; ISO 9001 Procurement Approval

business_glossary_term: Automatic Purchase Order (PO) Pricing Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this price list is enabled for automatic price determination during purchase order creation in the ERP system. When True, the system automatically applies this price list during PO generation without manual price entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Automatic Price Determination (OMFI); Procurement Automation Best Practices

description: The timestamp when this price list record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Audit Trail Requirements; ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price and all pricing tiers are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX conversion in cost-of-goods calculations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Currency (EINE-WAERS)

description: The agreed percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the net unit price. Represents the base-level trade discount negotiated with the supplier, separate from volume-tier discounts. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Condition (KONP-KBETR); CIPS Procurement Standards

description: The date from which this price list becomes valid and can be used for automated purchase order price determination and cost-of-goods calculations. Aligns with the validity start date in the ERP purchasing info record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Validity Period (EKPO-MEPRF); ISO 8601 Date Standard

business_glossary_term: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Purchasing Info Record Number
description: The SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record (PIR) number corresponding to this price list entry. Enables direct traceability between the Silver layer data product and the ERP system of record for procurement price determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (ME11/ME12/ME13); ERP Integration Standards

description: The date after which this price list is no longer valid for purchase order pricing. Null indicates an open-ended price list with no defined expiration. Used to trigger price list renewal workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Validity Period (EKPO-MEPRF); ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: Indicates whether freight/shipping costs are included in the quoted unit price. When True, no separate freight charge is added during PO creation. When False, freight is billed separately or calculated via the TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Freight Condition (FRA1); ICC Incoterms 2020; APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: Indicates whether this price list includes a volume discount schedule (quantity break tiers) in addition to the base unit price. When True, the associated supplier_price_list_tier records define the tiered pricing structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scale Pricing (Condition Scales); CIPS Volume Discount Standards

description: The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between the supplier and the e-commerce platform for shipments under this price list (e.g., FOB, DDP, EXW). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Incoterms (EKKO-INCO1); WTO International Trade Regulations

description: The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., 'Port of Shanghai' for FOB, 'Buyer Warehouse' for DDP). Required to fully specify the delivery terms per ICC Incoterms 2020 rules.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Incoterms Location (EKKO-INCO2)

description: The agreed supplier lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the warehouse. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and SLA monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Planned Delivery Time (EINE-WEBAZ); APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: The minimum quantity of the SKU that must be ordered per purchase order line to qualify for the agreed unit price. Orders below the MOQ may be rejected by the supplier or subject to a surcharge. Key procurement constraint for replenishment planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Minimum Order Quantity (EINE-MINBM); CIPS Procurement Standards

description: Free-text field for procurement team annotations, special conditions, or supplier-communicated caveats associated with this price list (e.g., 'Price valid only for orders placed before end of quarter', 'Subject to raw material surcharge above $X/tonne').
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Text; ISO 9001 Document Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country of origin for the SKU under this price list. Used for import duty calculation, customs compliance, trade restriction checks, and landed cost estimation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; WTO Rules of Origin; US CBP Import Regulations; EU Customs Code

description: The agreed payment terms code applicable to purchases under this price list (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Aligns with the payment terms configured in the ERP vendor master and accounts payable module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP - Payment Terms (T052); SOX Section 404 - Procurement Controls

description: Indicates whether the unit price represents the gross list price (before discounts), the net agreed price (after discounts), or the landed cost (inclusive of freight, duties, and insurance). Critical for correct COGS calculation and PO price validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Type (KONP); CIPS Cost Analysis Standards; APICS Landed Cost

description: Business category of the price list indicating its commercial purpose. 'standard' = regular agreed pricing; 'promotional' = time-limited promotional pricing; 'seasonal' = seasonal pricing adjustment; 'clearance' = end-of-life or excess inventory pricing; 'emergency' = expedited sourcing price.
reference: CIPS Procurement Category Management; SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Category

description: Human-readable name or description of the price list, such as 'Q1 2025 Electronics Pricing' or 'Annual Volume Agreement - Supplier XYZ'. Used for display and search in procurement portals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record; Procurement Best Practices

description: Externally-known business identifier for this price list, used in supplier communications, purchase order references, and ERP purchasing info records. Follows the format PL-{alphanumeric code}.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (ME11/ME12); ISO/IEC 11179

description: Classification of the price list by its commercial basis. 'contract_based' = tied to a formal vendor contract; 'standalone_pir' = standalone Purchasing Info Record (PIR) in ERP; 'spot_quote' = one-time quoted price; 'catalog' = supplier-published catalog price; 'blanket_order' = price under a blanket purchase order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Category; CIPS Procurement Standards

description: Version number of this price list, enabling tracking of successive revisions for the same supplier-SKU combination. Incremented each time the supplier submits a revised pricing schedule.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record versioning; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: The maximum allowable percentage deviation between the price list unit price and the actual invoice price before a three-way match exception is raised. Used in automated invoice verification to flag price discrepancies requiring manual review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP - Invoice Verification Tolerance (OMR6); SOX Section 404 - AP Controls; CIPS Invoice Matching Standards

description: The quantity basis for which the unit price is quoted (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units). Used to correctly compute per-unit cost when the supplier quotes prices in bulk multiples. Corresponds to the SAP Purchasing Info Record 'Price Unit' field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Price Unit (EINE-PEINH); GS1 Unit of Measure Standards

description: The number of days before the expiry date at which a renewal reminder notification should be triggered to the procurement team. Supports proactive price list renewal management and prevents procurement disruption due to expired pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Reminder; CIPS Contract Management Best Practices

description: The reference number of the source document from which this price list was derived, such as a supplier quotation number, RFQ response number, or EDI catalog transmission ID. Provides traceability back to the originating supplier communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Request for Quotation (ME41); EDI X12 843 Price/Sales Catalog; ISO 9001 Document Traceability

description: Current lifecycle state of the price list. 'active' = currently valid for PO pricing; 'expired' = past expiry date; 'superseded' = replaced by a newer version; 'draft' = under preparation; 'pending_approval' = awaiting procurement approval; 'suspended' = temporarily deactivated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Status; ISO 9001 Procurement Controls

description: The supplier's own part number or item code for this SKU, as used in the supplier's catalog and order acknowledgements. Required for EDI purchase order transmission and supplier invoice matching. Distinct from the internal SKU identifier.
reference: GS1 GTIN Standards; EDI X12 850 Purchase Order Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - Info Record Supplier Material Number (EINE-IDNLF)

description: The supplier's description of the SKU as it appears in their catalog or price list document. Used for three-way matching during invoice verification and for resolving discrepancies between internal and supplier item descriptions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Info Record Material Description (EINE-TXZ01); EDI X12 850 Standard

description: The tax classification code assigned to this SKU-supplier combination for VAT/GST determination during purchase order processing. Maps to the tax condition type in the ERP pricing procedure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Tax Indicator (EINE-MWSKZ); EU VAT Directive; GDPR (not applicable); FTC Consumer Protection

description: The number of quantity break tiers defined in the volume discount schedule for this price list. Zero if has_volume_tiers is False. Provides a quick summary of pricing complexity without joining to tier detail records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Scale Count; CIPS Volume Pricing Standards

description: The agreed supplier unit price for one unit of the SKU at the base quantity tier. This is the SSOT for supplier-agreed cost used in automated PO price validation and cost-of-goods (COGS) calculations. Does not represent the selling price or invoice amount.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Net Price (EINE-NETPR); PCI DSS (not applicable - non-payment data); CIPS Procurement Standards

description: The unit of measure in which the SKU is priced and ordered (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, L for litre). Aligns with the UOM defined in the product master and used in purchase order line items.
reference: GS1 Unit of Measure Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master UOM (MARA-MEINS); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: The timestamp when this price list record was last modified. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Audit Trail Requirements; ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the vendor contract record in the procurement data product. Primary key for the vendor_contract entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Outline Agreement (ME31K/ME32K)

description: Reference to the employee who provided final authorization and approval for this contract or its most recent amendment. Supports SOX audit trail requirements for procurement authorization controls.
reference: SOX Section 302/404 — Authorization Controls; SAP S/4HANA Workflow Approval

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for B2B contract management reports linking each supplier contract to the buying customer.account.

Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: B2B contract management requires identifying the specific buyer-side contact (procurement manager, category manager) who owns and is accountable for the vendor contract. vendor_contract has buyer_acco

Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Vendor contracts in e-commerce procurement are scoped to product categories (e.g., electronics, apparel). Category managers need to identify active contracts governing their category for sourcing deci

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor contracts are budget-controlled at cost center level. Procurement teams track which cost center owns contract spend for budget management, spend-against-budget reporting, and cost allocation. A

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Vendor contracts are executed by a specific legal entity — essential for legal compliance, VAT/tax obligations, intercompany accounting, and regulatory filings. In e-commerce with multi-entity structu

Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: In multi-marketplace e-commerce platforms, vendor contracts are scoped to a specific marketplace (e.g., supplier contracted for US marketplace but not EU). This FK enables marketplace-specific contrac

Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Contract management: contracts are negotiated between a specific seller and a supplier; linking enables contract compliance and SLA reporting per seller.
Reference to the supplier/vendor party who is the counterparty to this contract. Links to the vendor master record in the procurement domain.

Total number of formal amendments (change orders) that have been executed against this contract since its original execution. Provides a quick indicator of contract stability and negotiation history. Incremented each time a change order is approved.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Change History; CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management

description: Indicates whether the contract is configured to automatically renew upon reaching its expiry date without requiring a new negotiation cycle. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action. Critical for contract lifecycle management and budget planning.
reference: CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management; SAP S/4HANA Scheduling Agreement Renewal

description: Data classification level assigned to this contract document indicating the sensitivity of its commercial terms and conditions. Restricted: highly sensitive strategic terms. Confidential: standard commercial terms not for external disclosure. Internal: accessible to all internal staff. Public: terms disclosed publicly (rare for vendor contracts).
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Classification; GDPR Article 5 — Data Minimisation; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the vendor contract as assigned by the procurement or ERP system (e.g., SAP scheduling agreement or outline agreement number). Used in all vendor communications, purchase orders, and audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Outline Agreement Number (EKKO.EBELN); ISO 9001 Procurement Documentation

description: Current lifecycle state of the vendor contract governing its operational validity. Draft: under internal review and not yet binding. Active: fully executed and in force. Amended: modified via change order but still active. Suspended: temporarily halted pending resolution. Expired: reached end date without renewal. Terminated: ended before expiry by either party.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Release/Status Indicator; CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management

description: Descriptive human-readable title or short name of the vendor contract (e.g., 'Annual Packaging Materials Supply Agreement FY2025'). Used for display in procurement dashboards and contract registers.
reference: ISO 9001 Procurement Documentation; SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KDATB

description: Classification of the procurement contract by commercial structure. Framework agreements establish terms for future orders; blanket purchase orders cover recurring purchases up to a ceiling value; spot contracts are one-time buys; consignment contracts allow inventory held at buyer premises billed on consumption; scheduled agreements define delivery schedules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: framework|blanket|spot|consignment|scheduled — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Agreement Type (EKKO.BSART); CIPS Procurement Classification

description: The timestamp when this vendor contract record was first created in the procurement system (SAP S/4HANA or data lakehouse). Represents the system-of-record creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.AEDAT; SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail; ISO 8601

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the contract value, pricing, and payment obligations are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX exposure reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA EKKO.WAERS; PCI DSS — Financial Transaction Currency

description: Code identifying the designated delivery destination (warehouse, fulfillment center, or plant) where the vendor must deliver goods under this contract. Maps to the WMS facility or SAP S/4HANA plant/storage location.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Plant (EKKO.WERKS); WMS Facility Master

description: The contractually agreed mechanism for resolving commercial disputes between the buyer and vendor (e.g., binding arbitration, mediation, or court litigation). Determines the legal process to follow in case of contract breach or disagreement.
reference: ICC Arbitration Rules; WTO Dispute Settlement; UCC Article 2

description: The date on which the vendor contract becomes legally binding and commercially effective. Marks the start of the contract period for SLA enforcement, payment terms, and procurement eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KDATB (Agreement Start Date); UCC Article 2 — Contract Formation

description: Indicates whether this contract grants the vendor exclusive supply rights for the specified goods or services category, preventing the buyer from sourcing from competing vendors during the contract term. True = exclusive; False = non-exclusive.
reference: FTC — Exclusive Dealing Arrangements; CIPS Supplier Relationship Management

description: Free-text description of the scope of exclusivity when the exclusivity flag is True. Defines the product categories, geographic regions, or channels covered by the exclusive arrangement (e.g., 'Exclusive supplier of private-label packaging materials for APAC fulfillment centers').
reference: FTC — Exclusive Dealing Arrangements; EU Electronic Commerce Directive

description: The date on which the vendor contract is scheduled to expire and cease to be binding, unless renewed or extended. Nullable for open-ended evergreen contracts. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance alerts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KDATV (Agreement End Date); CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processing Agreement (DPA) Flag
description: Indicates whether a GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) has been executed with this vendor, required when the vendor processes personal data on behalf of the company. True = DPA in place; False = DPA not required or not yet executed. Mandatory for GDPR Article 28 compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 28 — Processor Obligations; CCPA Section 1798.100; ISO 27001

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this contract (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Critical for cross-border procurement and dispute resolution.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO International Trade Regulations; GDPR Article 3 — Territorial Scope

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the allocation of delivery costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and vendor. Governs logistics cost accounting and insurance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA EKKO.INCO1; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: The date on which the most recent formal amendment or change order to this contract was executed and became effective. Null for contracts that have never been amended. Used to track contract evolution and trigger re-approval workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Amendment History; SOX Section 404

description: Free-text description of the business reason for the most recent contract amendment (e.g., 'Price adjustment due to raw material cost increase Q3 2025', 'Extended term by 12 months following supplier performance review'). Supports audit trail and change management documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Reason; SOX Section 302 — Change Documentation; CIPS Contract Management

description: The contractually agreed number of calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the designated receiving location. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and WMS receiving scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Planned Delivery Time (EINE.PLIFZ); APICS Supply Chain Dictionary

description: The minimum quantity per purchase order that the vendor requires under this contract, as negotiated in the commercial terms. MOQ is a standard e-commerce and supply chain procurement metric used for inventory planning and replenishment decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Minimum Order Quantity (EINE.MINBM); CIPS Procurement Terms

business_glossary_term: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure applicable to the minimum order quantity (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, PAL for pallet). Follows ISO 80000 / UN/CEFACT unit codes as used in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20 — Units of Measure; SAP S/4HANA MEINS

description: Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is in place with this vendor as part of or alongside this contract. True = NDA executed; False = no NDA. Relevant for IP protection, proprietary pricing, and technology sharing arrangements.
reference: ISO 27001 — Confidentiality Agreements; GDPR Article 28 — Data Processing Agreements; NIST

description: The agreed method by which payments to the vendor will be made under this contract. Bank transfer (ACH/wire) is most common for B2B procurement; letter of credit is used for international suppliers; open account for trusted long-term vendors.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Payment Method (LFBK.ZWELS); PCI DSS — Payment Processing

description: Coded payment terms agreed with the vendor governing invoice due dates and early payment discounts (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment terms key (ZTERM) used in accounts payable processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Payment Terms (T052.ZTERM); UCC Article 2 — Payment Terms

description: The maximum cumulative financial penalty amount (in contract currency) that can be levied against the vendor under this contract over its full term. Caps total liability exposure for both parties. Null when no penalty cap is defined.
reference: CIPS Contract Management; UCC Article 2 — Limitation of Liability; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

description: Indicates whether this contract contains financial penalty clauses for SLA breaches, late delivery, or quality failures. True = penalty clauses exist; False = no financial penalties defined. Drives penalty calculation workflows in accounts payable.
reference: CIPS Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Penalty Processing; UCC Article 2

description: The contractually defined financial penalty rate expressed as a percentage of the affected order value or invoice amount, applied when the vendor breaches SLA commitments. Null when penalty_clause_flag is False.
reference: CIPS Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; UCC Article 2 — Liquidated Damages

description: Number of calendar days before the contract expiry date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Used to trigger automated procurement alerts and prevent unintended auto-renewals.
reference: CIPS Contract Management Best Practice; SAP S/4HANA Contract Reminder Configuration

description: The date on which the contract was formally executed (signed) by all authorized parties. May differ from the effective date when contracts are backdated or post-dated. Critical for legal enforceability and audit.
reference: UCC Article 2; SOX Section 302 — Contract Execution Records

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Maximum Defect Rate Percentage
description: Contractually agreed maximum acceptable defect/rejection rate (as a percentage) for goods delivered under this contract. Exceeding this threshold constitutes an SLA breach and may trigger return merchandise authorization (RMA) processes and financial penalties.
reference: CIPS SLA Framework; CPSC Product Safety Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Order Fill Rate Target Percentage
description: Contractually committed minimum order fill rate (as a percentage) representing the proportion of ordered quantity that the vendor must fulfill completely per shipment. A fill rate below this threshold triggers SLA breach notifications and potential penalties.
reference: CIPS SLA Framework; APICS Supply Chain Dictionary — Fill Rate; ISO 9001

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) On-Time Delivery Target Percentage
description: Contractually committed minimum on-time delivery rate (as a percentage) that the vendor must achieve. Measured as the percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the agreed delivery date. Used for vendor performance scorecards and penalty clause triggers.
reference: CIPS SLA Framework; SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Evaluation (ME61); ISO 9001 Supplier Performance

description: The actual date on which the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that expired naturally or are still active. Distinct from expiry_date which is the scheduled end; this captures unplanned or negotiated early termination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Termination; UCC Article 2 — Contract Termination; CIPS

description: Coded reason for early contract termination. Mutual agreement: both parties agreed to end early. Vendor breach: vendor failed to meet contractual obligations. Buyer breach: buyer defaulted. Force majeure: unforeseeable external event. Regulatory: compliance or legal requirement. Convenience: buyer exercised termination for convenience clause.
reference: UCC Article 2 — Contract Termination; CIPS Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The total maximum committed monetary value of the vendor contract over its full term, expressed in the contract currency. For blanket/framework agreements this is the ceiling value; for spot contracts it is the fixed order value. Used for spend analytics, budget commitment tracking, and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KTWRT (Target Value); SOX Section 404 — Procurement Spend Controls; CIPS Spend Analysis

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this vendor contract record in the procurement system. Updated on every change including amendments, status transitions, and field updates. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.AEDAT; SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail; ISO 8601; ETL Change Data Capture

description: Unique identifier for the customer account. Primary key for the account entity.
Reference to the customer's preferred shipping address for this account. Pre-populated at checkout for faster order placement.
Reference to the customer's preferred billing address for invoices and payment processing. May differ from shipping address for B2B accounts.
Reference to the dedicated account manager or customer success representative assigned to this account. Typically assigned to Enterprise B2B accounts and VIP customers.

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_profile. Business justification: Account belongs to a single Customer Profile; adding account.customer_profile_id creates the necessary parent‑child relationship and eliminates the orphaned Account side.
Reference to the customer's preferred payment method for this account. Used for one-click checkout and subscription billing.

Foreign key linking to payment.wallet. Business justification: Checkout wallet selection: e-commerce accounts need a designated default digital wallet for one-click checkout and balance-based payments. This FK supports wallet balance display at checkout, auto-deb

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: REQUIRED: Account‑level price list assignment for corporate customers; finance reports aggregate spend by account using the linked price list.
Externally-visible unique account number used for customer communication, statements, and support interactions.
Channel through which the account was originally created. Used for Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) analysis and channel attribution.

Current lifecycle status of the account. Active for operational accounts, Suspended for temporarily restricted accounts, Closed for permanently terminated accounts, Pending for accounts awaiting verification or approval.
reference: Customer Data Platform - Account Lifecycle Management

description: Service tier or membership level of the account determining benefits, pricing, and service levels. Standard for basic accounts, Premium for paid memberships, VIP for high-value customers, Enterprise for B2B corporate accounts.

Classification of the account based on the commercial relationship: B2C (Business to Consumer) for personal shopping accounts, B2B (Business to Business) for corporate procurement accounts, C2C (Consumer to Consumer) for marketplace seller accounts.
Legal company name for B2B corporate accounts. Null for B2C personal accounts. Used for invoicing, tax reporting, and corporate account management.
Date when the account was permanently closed. Null for active and suspended accounts. Used for churn analysis and retention reporting.

business_glossary_term: Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) Last Calculated Date
description: Date when the CLTV score was last recalculated. CLTV is typically refreshed monthly or quarterly based on updated transaction data.
reference: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - CLTV Refresh Cadence

description: Timestamp when this account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.
Maximum credit amount extended to the account for B2B corporate accounts or premium consumer accounts. Null for standard pay-per-order accounts. Used for order approval and risk management.
reference: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) - Credit Management

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit limit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Predicted total revenue expected from this account over its lifetime. Calculated by analytics models based on purchase history, frequency, and retention probability. Used for segmentation and marketing investment decisions.
reference: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - CLTV Modeling Standards

description: Indicates whether the account holder has consented to data processing for personalization, analytics, and AI/ML applications beyond core transaction processing. Required under GDPR.
reference: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 6 - Lawful Basis for Processing

description: Timestamp when the customer provided data processing consent. Required for GDPR compliance audit trails. Null if data_processing_consent is False.
reference: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 7 - Consent Records

description: Timestamp of the most recent successful login to the account. Used for engagement analysis, dormancy detection, and security monitoring.
Timestamp when this account record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and data synchronization.
Date of the most recent order placed by this account. Used for recency-frequency-monetary (RFM) analysis and churn prediction. Null if no orders have been placed.
reference: Order Management System (OMS) - Order History Tracking

description: Current balance of loyalty points or rewards currency available for redemption. Zero if not enrolled or no points earned.
reference: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - Loyalty Points Management

description: Indicates whether the account is enrolled in the company's loyalty or rewards program. True if enrolled, False otherwise.
reference: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - Loyalty Program Standards

description: Current tier level within the loyalty program based on spending or engagement. Null if not enrolled in loyalty program.
reference: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - Loyalty Tier Classification

description: Indicates whether the account holder has consented to receive marketing communications (emails, SMS, push notifications). Managed under GDPR and CCPA consent requirements.
reference: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 7 - Consent Management

description: Timestamp when the customer provided marketing consent. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance audit trails. Null if marketing_opt_in is False.
reference: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 7 - Consent Records

description: Indicates whether this account is also registered as a marketplace seller (C2C model). True if the account holder sells products on the platform, False for buyer-only accounts.
Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) rating provided by the account holder, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Used for customer satisfaction measurement and churn prediction.
reference: Net Promoter System - NPS Methodology Standards

description: Date when the most recent NPS survey response was collected. Null if the customer has never completed an NPS survey.
reference: Net Promoter System - Survey Administration Standards

description: Date when the account was first opened and activated. Used for calculating account tenure and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV).
Current outstanding balance owed by the account holder for credit-based accounts. Represents unpaid invoices and charges. Zero for prepaid accounts.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) - Accounts Receivable

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the customer's preferred pricing and billing currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency pricing display.
Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the customer's preferred language for communication, emails, and user interface (e.g., en, es, fr, de).
Referral or promotional code used during account registration. Used for tracking referral program effectiveness and attributing customer acquisition.
Fraud risk score assigned to the account by fraud detection systems, typically ranging from 0 (low risk) to 100 (high risk). Used for transaction approval, account monitoring, and fraud prevention.
reference: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) - Fraud Detection

description: Timestamp when the fraud risk score was last recalculated. Risk scores are updated in real-time or near-real-time based on transaction patterns and fraud signals.
reference: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) - Risk Assessment

description: Government-issued tax identification number for B2B accounts. Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., EIN in USA, CRN in UK). Used for tax reporting and compliance.
Lifetime count of orders placed by this account. Used for customer segmentation and loyalty tier calculation.
reference: Order Management System (OMS) - Order History Aggregation

description: VAT registration number for B2B accounts in VAT-applicable jurisdictions (primarily EU). Used for VAT exemption and cross-border invoicing.
reference: European Union VAT Directive - VAT Registration Standards

description: Unique identifier for the contact person. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Contact belongs to an account (B2B); many contacts per account, add FK to associate contact with its owning account.

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: In B2B e-commerce, individual contacts (buyers, procurement officers, decision-makers) associated with an account often have their own mailing, shipping, or billing addresses distinct from the account
Reference to the parent customer account (typically B2B enterprise account) that this contact is associated with. Enables multi-contact account management.
Reference to the contact_id of the person this contact reports to within the customer organization. Enables organizational hierarchy mapping and approval escalation workflows.
Maximum monetary amount (in account base currency) that this contact is authorized to approve for purchase orders without escalation. Used in B2B purchase approval workflows.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls, ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security Management

description: Email address of the contact's executive assistant. Used for meeting scheduling and communication coordination in high-value B2B accounts.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, RFC 5322 - Internet Message Format

description: Name of the contact's executive assistant or administrative support person. Used for scheduling and communication routing in enterprise B2B accounts.
Phone number of the contact's executive assistant. Used for urgent communication routing and meeting coordination.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, ITU-T E.164 - International Telephone Numbering

description: Level of authorization and permissions granted to the contact within the customer account. Determines purchase approval limits, account management capabilities, and data access rights.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 - Security and Privacy Controls, ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security Management

description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts can receive communications and perform authorized actions; inactive contacts are retained for historical reference but cannot transact.

Classification of the contact role within the account structure. Determines routing of specific communication types and approval workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|billing|shipping|technical|procurement|finance|legal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the system. Used for contact tenure analysis and audit trail.
reference: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls

description: Timestamp when the contact was deactivated or marked inactive. Null for active contacts. Used for contact lifecycle analysis and retention reporting.
Free-text explanation of why the contact was deactivated (e.g., left company, role change, duplicate record, customer request). Used for contact churn analysis and data quality improvement.

Department or business unit within the customer organization that the contact belongs to (e.g., Procurement, Finance, Operations, IT). Enables department-level segmentation and targeted communications.
Primary email address for the contact. Used for order confirmations, purchase approvals, shipping notifications, and targeted B2B communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, RFC 5322 - Internet Message Format

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive marketing and promotional email communications. Managed under GDPR consent requirements and CAN-SPAM Act compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 6 - Lawfulness of Processing, CAN-SPAM Act 15 USC 7704

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact's email address has been verified through a confirmation process. Used for security and communication deliverability assurance.
Contact identifier from the source system or external CRM platform. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration mapping.
First name or given name of the contact person. Used for personalized communication and identification within B2B account structures.

Boolean flag indicating whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the customer account. Primary contacts receive all general account communications and have highest authorization level.
Job title or role of the contact within their organization (e.g., Procurement Manager, Finance Director, Shipping Coordinator). Used for routing communications and approvals to the appropriate role.
Timestamp of the contact's most recent login to the customer portal or B2B platform. Used for engagement tracking and inactive contact identification.
Last name or family name of the contact person. Combined with first name for full contact identification.
URL to the contact's LinkedIn professional profile. Used for B2B relationship management, account intelligence, and professional network mapping.
Mobile phone number for the contact. Used for SMS notifications, two-factor authentication, and mobile-first communication channels.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, ITU-T E.164 - International Telephone Numbering

description: Primary phone number for the contact. Used for order clarifications, delivery coordination, and urgent account communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, ITU-T E.164 - International Telephone Numbering

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive phone call communications for marketing purposes. Managed under TCPA compliance and do-not-call registry requirements.
reference: TCPA 47 USC 227, FTC Telemarketing Sales Rule

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact's phone number has been verified through SMS or voice confirmation. Used for two-factor authentication and fraud prevention.
Contact's preferred method of communication for non-urgent account matters. Used to optimize communication delivery and improve contact engagement rates.

Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the contact's preferred language for communications (e.g., EN for English, ES for Spanish, FR for French). Used for localized email templates and customer service routing.
Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive SMS text message communications. Managed under TCPA compliance and GDPR consent requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 6 - Lawfulness of Processing, TCPA 47 USC 227

description: Name of the operational system that originated this contact record (e.g., Customer Data Platform, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Seller Management Portal). Used for data lineage tracking and system integration management.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 - Metadata Registries, DAMA-DMBOK Data Lineage

description: IANA time zone identifier for the contact's location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling communications and calls during appropriate business hours.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database, ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format

description: Timestamp when the contact record was last modified. Used for data freshness tracking and change audit.
reference: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls

description: Unique identifier for the address record. Primary key.
Reference to the customer profile that owns this address. Links address to unified customer view in Customer Data Platform (CDP).
reference: Customer Data Platform - Unified Customer Profile

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: Address serviceability check at checkout: the system must determine which delivery zone a customer address falls into to display accurate SLA, apply residential/remote surcharges, and validate carrier

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Customer addresses determine applicable price zones for geographic pricing, regional tax rates, and delivery surcharges. E-commerce pricing engines resolve the correct price list by mapping a customer

Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active = currently usable, inactive = temporarily disabled, archived = historical record, flagged = requires review due to delivery failures or fraud indicators.
reference: Customer Data Platform - Address Lifecycle Management

description: Classification of address purpose: shipping (delivery destination), billing (invoice address), registered (legal/account address), pickup (BOPIS location), return (RMA destination).
City or municipality name for the address. Used for delivery routing and tax jurisdiction determination.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard, GDPR Article 4

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has consented to receive marketing communications at this address. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance. Used by Marketing Automation Platform for campaign targeting.
reference: GDPR Article 6 - Lawful Basis for Processing, CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Timestamp when marketing consent was granted or last updated for this address. Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 7 - Conditions for Consent, CCPA

description: Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country of the address. Used for international shipping, customs, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR for EU countries).
Timestamp when this address record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR compliance (right to access). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, GDPR Article 15

description: Special delivery instructions provided by the customer for this address (e.g., gate code, leave at door, ring bell, call upon arrival). Used by last-mile delivery carriers and 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) providers.
Number of failed delivery attempts to this address. Used for address quality scoring and to flag problematic addresses. High count triggers address verification workflow.
Date when this address was first used for an order or transaction. Used for address age analysis and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) modeling.

Precision level of the geocoded coordinates. Rooftop = exact building location, range_interpolated = estimated within address range, geometric_center = center of postal code area, approximate = city/region level. Impacts delivery time estimation accuracy.
reference: Google Geocoding API Standards, OpenStreetMap Geocoding Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) Eligible Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this address is eligible for BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) fulfillment. True if address is within serviceable radius of a store or pickup location with inventory availability.
reference: BOPIS Fulfillment Standards, Omnichannel Retail Guidelines
